name: subtract_mod_eq_zero_external_body
code:
proof fn phi_1_subtract_mod_eq_zero_external_body(a: nat, b: nat, c: nat)
    requires
        aligned(a, c),
        aligned(b, c),
        a <= b,
        c > 0,
    ensures
        aligned((b - a) as nat, c),
{
    subtract_mod_eq_zero(a, b, c);
}
reason: `subtract_mod_eq_zero` is `external_body` — closure of alignment under subtraction is trusted without proof

name: div_mul_cancel_external_body
reason: `div_mul_cancel` is `external_body` — the division-multiplication roundtrip for aligned values is trusted without proof

name: aligned_addr_equals_entry_base
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_addr_equals_entry_base(base: nat, addr: nat, entry_size: nat, num_entries: nat)
    requires
        addr >= base,
        addr < entry_base_from_index(base, num_entries, entry_size),
        entry_size > 0,
        aligned(base, entry_size),
        aligned(addr, entry_size),
    ensures
        addr == entry_base_from_index(base, index_from_base_and_addr(base, addr, entry_size), entry_size),
{
    lemma_index_from_base_and_addr(base, addr, entry_size, num_entries);
}
reason: An aligned address equals the entry base at its index — depends on both `subtract_mod_eq_zero` and `div_mul_cancel` external_body axioms

name: index_in_bounds
reason: The computed index is always within bounds — verified by nonlinear_arith without external_body dependency

name: addr_between_entry_bases
code:
proof fn phi_5_addr_between_entry_bases(base: nat, addr: nat, entry_size: nat, num_entries: nat)
    requires
        addr >= base,
        addr < entry_base_from_index(base, num_entries, entry_size),
        entry_size > 0,
    ensures
        between(
            addr,
            entry_base_from_index(base, index_from_base_and_addr(base, addr, entry_size), entry_size),
            next_entry_base_from_index(base, index_from_base_and_addr(base, addr, entry_size), entry_size),
        ),
{
    lemma_index_from_base_and_addr(base, addr, entry_size, num_entries);
}
reason: Any address falls between consecutive entry bases at its index — verified by nonlinear_arith, tests the floor-division localization property
